data.title: USDT Supply Declines Amid Regulatory Changes and Market Downturn
data.description: USDT, the largest stablecoin, has experienced a supply reduction of approximately 1.7% over the past month, marking the most significant decrease since the collapse of FTX. According to NS3.AI, this decline is attributed to the European Union's implementation of the Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) regulations and a broader downturn in the cryptocurrency market. The reduced supply indicates a decrease in demand and highlights the impact of regulatory uncertainty on market dynamics.

data.title: Hong Kong's Deputy Secretary for Financial Services and the Treasury: Building Hong Kong into a Regional Gold Reserve Hub
data.description: According to reports, at the Hong Kong Gold Exchange's Lunar New Year Gold Opening Ceremony and 2026 Lunar New Year Opening Ceremony, Chan Ho-lim, Under Secretary for Financial Services and the Treasury of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region Government, stated that in order to further diversify Hong Kong's international financial business, the SAR government is making every effort to promote Hong Kong as an international gold trading center. Chan said that the goal is to expand gold storage in Hong Kong to over 2,000 tons within three years, making Hong Kong a regional gold reserve hub. Regarding the promotion of commodity trading, there are currently 15 London Metal Exchange-approved warehouses operating in Hong Kong. The Hong Kong Gold Exchange will also deepen its connectivity with the Guangzhou Futures Exchange and other commodity markets in mainland China. (Xinhua)

data.title: AI Data Center Expansion Could Accelerate with 'Bring Your Own Capacity' Approach
data.description: The concept of 'Bring Your Own Capacity' could potentially expedite the development of AI data centers, according to Tom W. Black. Bloomberg posted on X, emphasizing that this strategy should not lead to increased electricity costs for residential consumers. The approach involves leveraging existing infrastructure to enhance data center capabilities without necessitating new construction, thereby reducing time and resources required for expansion. However, careful consideration must be given to ensure that residential electricity prices remain unaffected by these developments. The balance between technological advancement and consumer impact remains a critical factor in the implementation of this strategy.

data.title: U.S. Ambassador Criticizes New EU Trade Rules
data.description:
U. S. Ambassador Andrew Puzder has expressed concerns over upcoming European Union trade regulations, describing them as a violation of the trade agreement established between the United States and the EU last year. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Puzder's remarks about the potential impact of these rules on the bilateral trade relationship
source: https://www.binance.com/en/square/post/293653416280818?utm_source=BinanceNewsRSS

data.title: Is Smart Money Accumulating XRP at These Levels?
data.description:
Exchange reserve data is often one of the clearest ways to understand what investors are actually doing, beyond price action. When exchange reserves rise sharply, it usually means holders are transferring assets to trading platforms, most often with the intention of selling. Coins become immediately liquid once deposited, increasing short-term selling pressure
source: https://www.ethnews.com/is-smart-money-accumulating-xrp-at-these-levels/

data.title: Ethereum Price Movements Could Trigger Significant Liquidations
data.description: Ethereum's price fluctuations are poised to impact liquidation levels significantly. According to ChainCatcher, Coinglass data indicates that if Ethereum's price falls below $1,872, the cumulative liquidation intensity for long positions across major centralized exchanges could reach $903 million. Conversely, if Ethereum surpasses $2,050, the cumulative liquidation intensity for short positions could amount to $547 million.

data.title: Starbucks Aims to Capture Afternoon Market to Boost Investor Confidence
data.description: Starbucks is experiencing a resurgence in its morning customer base, according to a report by the Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X. The coffee giant is now focusing on expanding its appeal during the afternoon hours to attract investors. The company has been implementing strategies to enhance its afternoon offerings, aiming to increase foot traffic and sales beyond the morning rush. Starbucks' efforts include introducing new menu items and promotions tailored to afternoon consumers. By strengthening its presence in the afternoon market, Starbucks hopes to improve its overall financial performance and regain investor trust. The company's strategy reflects a broader trend in the food and beverage industry, where businesses are seeking to maximize revenue throughout the day.

data.title: Economist Warns of Globalization and Populism's Negative Impact
data.description: Economist Eswar Prasad has highlighted the adverse effects of globalization and populism, suggesting that these forces exacerbate each other's negative impacts. Bloomberg posted on X, emphasizing Prasad's concerns about the intertwined relationship between these global phenomena. Prasad argues that globalization, while fostering economic growth, can also lead to inequality and social unrest, which populist movements often exploit. This cycle, described as a 'doom loop,' poses significant challenges to global stability and economic progress. The economist's analysis underscores the need for balanced policies to mitigate these risks and promote sustainable development.

data.title: U.S. Ambassador Criticizes New EU Trade Rules
data.description: U.S. Ambassador Andrew Puzder has expressed concerns over upcoming European Union trade regulations, describing them as a violation of the trade agreement established between the United States and the EU last year. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Puzder's remarks about the potential impact of these rules on the bilateral trade relationship. The ambassador emphasized that the new regulations contradict both the terms and the spirit of the previously negotiated deal. The situation underscores ongoing tensions in international trade relations, as both parties navigate the complexities of maintaining agreements while addressing domestic policy changes.

data.title: Long-Term Bitcoin Holders Just Turned Extremely Active
data.description:
Bitcoin’s Long-Term Holders (LTHs) have been more active in this cycle than at any comparable point in previous market expansions. After analyzing the Coin Days Destroyed (CDD) heatmap, it becomes visible that accumulated holding days are being spent at elevated levels relative to past cycles.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/long-term-bitcoin-holders-just-turned-extremely-active/

data.title: European Stocks Set for Record Monthly Inflows in February
data.description: European stocks are on track to achieve their highest-ever monthly inflows in February, as reported by EPFR, a firm that monitors ETF and mutual fund flows. The Long View, institutional investor, posted on X. This follows two consecutive weeks of record inflows, each amounting to approximately $10 billion. The surge in investments highlights growing investor confidence in the European market.

data.title: EssilorLuxottica Faces Significant Weekly Decline
data.description: EssilorLuxottica shares are experiencing their steepest weekly decline in nearly four years. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the company's challenging market performance. The eyewear giant, known for its popular brands, is witnessing a downturn that has caught the attention of investors and analysts alike. This decline comes amid broader market fluctuations and economic uncertainties affecting various sectors. The company's stock performance is being closely monitored as stakeholders assess potential impacts on future growth and profitability.

data.title: Poland's Nuclear Ambitions Attract Interest from Solorz Family
data.description: The children of billionaire Zygmunt Solorz are exploring opportunities for their energy unit to participate in Poland's upcoming nuclear power plant project. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the family's interest in contributing to the nation's nuclear energy expansion. This move aligns with Poland's broader strategy to diversify its energy sources and reduce reliance on coal. The Solorz family's involvement could bring significant investment and expertise to the project, potentially accelerating its development. Poland's government has been actively seeking partners for its nuclear initiatives, aiming to enhance energy security and meet environmental targets.

data.title: Michael Saylor's Strategic Shift Amid Bitcoin Price Decline
data.description: Michael Saylor has responded to the recent decline in Bitcoin prices by adopting a bold financial strategy. According to NS3.AI, Saylor is issuing substantial amounts of preferred stock in an effort to mitigate losses associated with Bitcoin investments. This strategy involves considerable financial risks, particularly related to leverage and market volatility.

data.title: SpaceX Falcon 9 Rocket Debris Causes Lithium Spike in Atmosphere
data.description: German researchers have published a study in the journal Communications Earth & Environment, revealing that a year ago, debris from a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket significantly increased lithium atom concentrations in the upper atmosphere. According to Jin10, this marks the first time scientists have measured environmental pollution caused by space debris re-entering the atmosphere. The findings are significant as advancements in space exploration continue to increase the amount of space debris.

data.title: South Korean Airlines Ban Power Banks in Cabins Due to Fire Risk
data.description: South Korean airlines have announced a comprehensive ban on the use of power banks within aircraft cabins. According to Jin10, this decision, reported on February 20, aims to mitigate the risk of fires and explosions associated with these devices. The move reflects growing concerns over the safety of portable electronic devices during flights. The ban applies to all airlines operating in South Korea, highlighting the industry's proactive approach to passenger safety.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | West Faces Challenges in Securing Critical Minerals, Says US Ambassador
data.description: US Ambassador to the EU Andrew Puzder has expressed concerns about the West's ability to access critical minerals independently. Bloomberg posted on X that Puzder emphasized the potential disadvantage the West could face without securing these essential resources. He also conveyed optimism about reaching a rare earths agreement in the near future.
